metadata.title: 🚀 Getting Started with This Template
metadata.description: **Note:** Steps 2-4 apply to v1.0.0 when working code is available. Currently (v0.1.0), use the documentation as your reference guide.

structure.heading: Choose Your Path
structure.summary: **First:** This is a **GitHub Template Repository**. [Learn about templates →](./TEMPLATE_USAGE.md)

subsections.heading: 📱 [Mobile-First Application](./docs/paths/mobile-first-app.md)
subsections.summary: iOS/Android apps with Expo • 3-4 weeks to MVP

subsections.heading: 🌐 [Web-First Application](./docs/paths/web-first-app.md)
subsections.summary: React web apps with Vite • 2-3 weeks to MVP

subsections.heading: 🚀 [Full-Stack Application](./docs/paths/full-stack-app.md)
subsections.summary: Mobile + Web platforms • 4-6 weeks to MVP

subsections.heading: 🏥 [Compliance-Heavy Application](./docs/paths/compliance-heavy-app.md)
subsections.summary: Healthcare, finance, regulated industries • 5-8 weeks to MVP

subsections.heading: ⚡ [Quick MVP](./docs/paths/quick-mvp.md)
subsections.summary: Rapid prototyping and validation • 1-2 weeks to prototype

subsections.heading: ⚡ Path A: Quick Start (Code-First)
subsections.summary: **Best for:** Small projects, prototypes, or developers who prefer to start coding immediately.

subsections.heading: 📝 Path B: PRD-First Workflow (Recommended)
subsections.summary: **Best for:** Complex projects, team collaboration, or when you need stakeholder alignment.

subsections.heading: Step 1: Create Your Repository from Template
subsections.summary: **Click the "Use this template" button** on GitHub or visit:

structure.heading: Step 3: Setup Development
structure.summary: **Once PRD is approved, follow the [Quick Start steps above](#-quick-start-code-first).**

structure.heading: Step 4: Build According to PRD
structure.summary: Use your PRD as the source of truth:
keyPoints: Implement features in priority order (P0 → P1 → P2)

codeBlocks.code:
pnpm build              # Build all apps
pnpm build:mobile       # Build mobile
pnpm build:web          # Build web

pnpm nx graph           # View dependency graph
pnpm nx affected:test   # Test affected projects
pnpm nx affected:build  # Build affected projects

structure.heading: 🎯 Success Checklist
structure.summary: Before starting development, ensure you have:

structure.heading: 💡 Pro Tips
keyPoints: **Start small:** Build MVP first, add features later
**Stay organized:** Keep PRD updated as requirements change
**Test early:** Write tests as you build features
**Ship often:** Use EAS Update for rapid iteration
**Get feedback:** Beta test before public launch
